title: The Shofar: Ancient Horn That Calls the Soul to Wake Up
title_zh: 羊角号(Shofar):唤醒灵魂的古老号角
title_he: השופר: קרן עתיקה המעוררת את הנשמה
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers, no background in Judaism
chinese_parallels: 警钟 (warning bell) - spiritual wake-up call
编钟和鼓 (bronze bells and drums) - ritual instruments connecting heaven and earth
禅宗顿悟 (Chan/Zen sudden awakening) - spiritual awakening moment
鸣金收兵 (sounding gongs to recall troops) - signaling important transitions
primary_sources: Numbers 29:1 - Biblical commandment to blow shofar
Rambam, Mishneh Torah, Laws of Repentance 3:4
Rambam, Mishneh Torah, Laws of Shofar 1-4
learning_objectives: Understand what a shofar is and how it's made
Learn the three types of shofar sounds: tekiah, shevarim, teruah
Understand the multiple symbolic meanings of the shofar
Learn when and why the shofar is blown during the Jewish year
Appreciate the shofar as a spiritual wake-up call for self-examination
Recognize cultural parallels between Jewish and Chinese traditions regarding ritual sounds
cultural_sensitivity_notes: Article draws parallels to Chinese ritual instruments (bells, drums, gongs)
References Chan/Zen Buddhist concepts of sudden awakening
Explains Chinese idioms related to warning bells and alarms
Mentions Chinese philosophical concepts from the Book of Rites (礼记)
Respectful comparison of ritual sound traditions
